summary: coroutineScope causes cascading cancellation when one child fails, losing partially-sent notifications.

findings.evidence: `BatchNotificationService.sendAll` and `sendUrgent` use `coroutineScope` to launch parallel children; if any child throws, all sibling coroutines are cancelled, so notifications already in-flight for other users are lost.
findings.minimal_fix: Replace `coroutineScope` with `supervisorScope` in both `sendAll` and `sendUrgent` so that a single child failure does not cancel siblings; collect failures into a list and throw an aggregate exception after all children complete.
findings.verification: Add a test where one `send()` call throws; assert that `send()` was still invoked for all remaining user IDs.

findings.evidence: There is no test covering partial failure: when one notification fails, the service should still deliver to the remaining recipients, but this behavior is untested.
findings.minimal_fix: Add a test that stubs `sender.send()` to throw for one specific userId and verifies that all other userIds still received the notification.
findings.verification: Run the partial-failure test and assert the sender was called for every userId except the failing one, and verify the aggregate exception contains the original failure.
